tossups.question: A self-taught artist from this country made lithographs like “Colossus” as a leader of the “Rupture Movement.”A regional text inspired a large, orange-toned painting held in this country in which a rainbow arcs over a “magical world.” In a self-portrait from this country, the artist sits before a white rocking-horse and holds her hand out to a hyena. An artist from this country included a dead woodpecker lying on a tree in a self-portrait that only shows the artist’s legs and feet in a bathtub. This country was the adopted home of the surrealist Leonora Carrington. An artist from this country referenced her teenage injury in a traffic accident by painting herself with a broken column for a spine. For 10 points, a distinctive unibrow appears in self-portraits by what country’s artist Frida Kahlo?
tossups.question_sanitized: A self-taught artist from this country made lithographs like "Colossus" as a leader of the "Rupture Movement."A regional text inspired a large, orange-toned painting held in this country in which a rainbow arcs over a "magical world." In a self-portrait from this country, the artist sits before a white rocking-horse and holds her hand out to a hyena. An artist from this country included a dead woodpecker lying on a tree in a self-portrait that only shows the artist's legs and feet in a bathtub. This country was the adopted home of the surrealist Leonora Carrington. An artist from this country referenced her teenage injury in a traffic accident by painting herself with a broken column for a spine. For 10 points, a distinctive unibrow appears in self-portraits by what country's artist Frida Kahlo?
tossups.answer: Mexico [or the United Mexican States or Estados Unidos Mexicanos] (The first clue refers to José Luis Cuevas. The second clue is Leonora Carrington’s The Magical World of the Mayas. The two Kahlo self-portraits are What the Water Gave Me and The Broken Column.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Mexico [or the United Mexican States or Estados Unidos Mexicanos] (The first clue refers to Jose Luis Cuevas. The second clue is Leonora Carrington's The Magical World of the Mayas. The two Kahlo self-portraits are What the Water Gave Me and The Broken Column.)

packet.name: G - Cambridge A, Iowa A, Minnesota A, Ohio State A, UCLA B, Waterloo B

tossups.question: A 1938 exhibition of this artist’s works in New York City arranged by André Breton led him to describe them as “a ribbon around a bomb.” A painting by this artist features a tree that is dwarfed by the dead bird resting on it. Isamu Noguchi removed the dedication on a painting by this artist commissioned by Clare Boothe Luce that depicts an American socialite in a pool of blood at the foot of a clouded skyscraper. The lifelong pain that this artist experienced due to a traffic accident at age 18 is represented by nails impaling her in The Broken Column and by a severed vein spilling blood onto her white dress in a double self-portrait. The Suicide of Dorothy Hale is a work of, for 10 points, what Mexican surrealist whose many other self-portraits depict her prominent unibrow?
tossups.answer: Frida Kahlo [or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderón] (The second clue is from What the Water Gave Me.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Frida Kahlo [or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderon] (The second clue is from What the Water Gave Me.)

tossups.question: The pose of two lesbian lovers in this painting is echoed in the artist’s later painting Two Nudes in the Forest. At this painting’s center-right, a happy-looking skeleton chills out on a hill. In this painting, various insects crawl along a tightrope which wraps around the neck of a dead woman and around the wrist of a man with a mask-like face. In this painting, a tree which is dwarfed by the dead bird lying on top of it stands next to a (*) skyscraper emerging from a smoldering volcano. Two feet, one of which is bloody and deformed, jut out from the grey water in this painting, which lends its name to a Florence and the Machine song. For 10 points, name this Frida Kahlo painting in which a bunch of objects float in a bathtub.
tossups.answer: What the Water Gave Me [or Lo que el agua me dio; or What I Saw in the Water]
tossups.answer_sanitized: What the Water Gave Me [or Lo que el agua me dio; or What I Saw in the Water]

bonuses.leadin: This artist painted Henry Ford Hospital while recovering from a miscarriage. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Mexican artist known for her many self portraits. Her rough marriage with Diego Rivera often influenced her art.

Rivera is notably not present in this Kahlo painting which operates as a memoir of her life. A skyscraper is erupting from a volcano in this painting which also depicts a married couple who are believed to be Kahlo's parents.
Echoing Kahlo's wish to possess him despite his many affairs, paintings such as Self-portrait as a Tehuana and Diego and I placed portraits of Diego Rivera's face at this unusual location.
answers: Frida Kahlo de Rivera [or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderón]
What the Water Gave Me [or What I Saw in the Water; or Lo que el agua me dio]
answers_sanitized: Frida Kahlo de Rivera [or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderon]
What the Water Gave Me [or What I Saw in the Water; or Lo que el agua me dio]
Kahlo's forehead [prompt on her body or her face]

Rivera is notably not present in this Kahlo painting which operates as a memoir of her life. A skyscraper is erupting from a volcano in this painting which also depicts a married couple who are believed to be Kahlo's parents.
Echoing Kahlo's wish to possess him despite his many affairs, paintings such as Self-portrait as a Tehuana and Diego and I placed portraits of Diego Rivera's face at this unusual location.

bonuses.leadin: Images in this painting include a dead canary on top of a gnarled tree, a naked woman being choked by a rope, and a skyscraper rising out of the mouth of a volcano. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this autobiographical painting that shows the artist’s bare legs in a bathtub where macabre images are floating on the surface.
What the Water Gave Me was painted by this Mexican artist, whose many surreal self-portraits often depict her iconic unibrow. She was married to Diego Rivera.

Clare Boothe Luce commissioned Kahlo to paint a portrait commemorating the suicide of this mutual friend. The painting is full of cottony clouds and shows this woman falling from Hampshire House onto the sidewalk.
answers: What the Water Gave Me [or What I Saw In the Water; or Lo Que el Agua Me Dio]
